question: Can you explain the four main principles of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) - Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, and Abstraction - and provide a simple Java example for one of them?

scores.feedback: You provided accurate definitions for Encapsulation, Inheritance, and Abstraction. The explanation for Polymorphism was mostly correct but could be slightly more precise. The most critical area for improvement is addressing all parts of the question; you omitted the request for a simple Java example. To enhance your fluency, focus on smoother transitions between ideas and a more natural sentence flow rather than just listing key terms. Practicing your explanations out loud can also boost your confidence.

transcript: The four main principles of object-oriented programming or encapsulation inheritance, polymorphism and abstraction, encapsulation, bundles, data with methods, operate on the data and hides the internal implementation details. Inheritance allows a class to acquire properties and methods from an existing class, prompting reuse. Polymorphism means many forms enabling a single interface to represent different underlying forms or an object to respond. Finally, an abstraction involves showing only the essential features of the object while hiding the complex irrelevant background ideas.
